instruments: SFC Circular 26EC31 (SFC-authorised funds with virtual asset exposure)
Code on Unit Trusts and Mutual Funds (UT Code)
standing_summary: Spot virtual asset ETFs (bitcoin and ether) have traded on HKEX since 30 April 2024, when Asia's first such products launched. A spot Solana ETF began trading around 27 October 2025. The SFC's governing circular for SFC-authorised funds with virtual asset exposure was updated on 27 May 2026 (Circular 26EC31, superseding the original December 2023 framework), and on 20 April 2026 the SFC introduced a new framework allowing 24/7 secondary trading of tokenised SFC-authorised investment products on licensed virtual asset trading platforms, with an initial focus on tokenised money-market funds. Separately, the standing UT Code is under a broader modernisation consultation (22 October 2025 to 21 January 2026) not specific to virtual assets.

key_links.label: SFC Circular 26EC31: SFC-authorised funds with exposure to virtual assets (27 May 2026)
SFC press release 26PR59: secondary trading of tokenised investment products (20 Apr 2026)
SFC Circular 23EC65: original VA fund exposure framework (22 Dec 2023)

open_items: Conclusions to the UT Code modernisation consultation (closed 21 Jan 2026) had not been located as published at the time of this research.
Whether the tokenised-fund secondary-trading pilot expands beyond money-market funds is not yet confirmed.
No evidence was found of additional spot virtual asset ETFs beyond bitcoin, ether, and Solana.
